entities.name: TROPICAL AGRO-ECOSYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural environments in tropical regions characterized by specific soil and climate conditions

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality and productivity in tropical agriculture

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for sustainable crop and livestock production

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The wearing away of topsoil, contributing to land degradation

entities.name: NUTRIENT MINING
entities.description: The removal of nutrients from the soil faster than they are replaced

entities.name: INORGANIC FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Chemically manufactured fertilizers used to supplement soil nutrients

entities.name: INTEGRATED SOIL FERTILITY MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An approach combining fertilizers, organic resources, and agronomic practices to improve yields and preserve soil fertility

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The amount of agricultural produce harvested per unit area

entities.name: RECYCLED ORGANIC RESOURCES
entities.description: The use of organic materials to restore and maintain soil fertility

entities.name: IMPROVED AGRONOMIC PRACTICES
entities.description: Enhanced farming techniques designed to minimize nutrient losses and improve efficiency

entities.name: NUTRIENT-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which crops utilize available nutrients

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where on-station and on-farm soil fertility studies were conducted

entities.name: ORGANIC FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Natural fertilizers derived from organic sources used to increase crop yields

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession to improve soil health and yield

entities.name: GREEN MANURING
entities.description: The practice of planting specific crops to be plowed back into the soil to improve fertility

entities.name: CROP RESIDUE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The handling of crop remains to improve soil fertility and yield

entities.name: MANURE
entities.description: Animal waste used as an organic fertilizer to increase grain yields

entities.name: NP FERTILIZER
entities.description: Nitrogen and Phosphorus fertilizers used to increase grain yields

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal grain crop whose yield is increased by manure and NP fertilizer

entities.name: FABA BEAN
entities.description: A legume crop whose yield is increased by manure and NP fertilizer

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUMES
entities.description: Leguminous plants used in rotation to increase cereal grain yields

entities.name: CEREAL
entities.description: Grass-based grain crops whose yields are increased by rotation with grain legumes

entities.name: ORGANIC RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant or animal materials left after harvest, often used for livestock feed and energy

entities.name: ECONOMIC BENEFITS
entities.description: Financial gains that serve as the main incentive for farmers to adopt ISFM practices
